exceptions.rule: Off-brand visual pattern — non-MH palette colors
exceptions.detailPattern: Patriotic red-to-blue gradient border (red-600 → blue-700) wrapping WA DVA Veteran Owned Business certification logo
exceptions.reason: The WA DVA certification logo has a non-transparent background and requires a container. The patriotic red/blue color scheme is inseparable from the meaning and authenticity of the Veteran Owned certification. Exception is strictly scoped to WaVobBadge component only.

Third-party chamber and accreditation logos use proper-noun capitalization (Kennewick TriCity Regional Chamber, Pasco Chamber, Richland Chamber). Renaming would break references to official organization assets. Pre-existing files migrated to monorepo path.
AGC (Associated General Contractors) is an acronym-based proper noun. The filename mirrors the official AGC branding. Pre-existing file migrated to monorepo path.

Vendor logo files use underscore-separated names that were established before the kebab-case convention. Renaming would require updating all component references. Pre-existing files migrated to monorepo path.
Document assets include AGC and Travelers Insurance logos which are proper-noun branded files. Pre-existing files migrated to monorepo path.
Test file for safety navigation contracts. Plain-language navigation labels are not appropriate in unit test assertion files; their presence is validated by the tests themselves.
Barrel re-export file for navigation components. Navigation labels are defined in the referenced modules, not the barrel export.
TypeScript interface definitions for navigation types. Plain-language nav labels belong in runtime configs, not type definition files.
Analytics metadata collection module collects device/browser/session data. It is not a navigation or SEO label registry; plain-language labels are not applicable here.
